rulings.Case Title: SFBA INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC VS. JAYDIPSINH DEVDHARA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jun 23, 2021 line 1. PETITIONER SFBA INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC MOTION TO STRIKE is DENIED. Respondent's Petition is not an improper motion for reconsideration. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SFBA INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC VS. JAYDIPSINH DEVDHARA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash May 7, 2021 Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award, Notice Of Hearing, And All Other Papers Served And Filed On May 7, 2021, And Objecting To Granting Of Any Other Relief

Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SFBA INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC VS. JAYDIPSINH DEVDHARA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Devdharas' Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: RESPONDENT JAYDIPSINH DEVDHARA, VIKRAMSINH DEVDHARA Notice Of Hearing On Devdharas' Petition Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jun 23, 2021 line 1. To Confirm Arbitration Award is GRANTED as follows: (1) Respondent's Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award attached as exhibit 8.c. to Petitioner's Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award is GRANTED; (2) Respondent's request in the Respondent's Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award to deny SFBA's petition as it pertains to "other relief" listed in Attachment 10, g to Petitioner's Petition to Confirm Arbitration award is not properly before this Court on this Petition and must be addressed separately before this entire action may be reduced to a judgment. Therefore, July 1, 2020 award issued by Hon. Robert A. Baines is confirmed and any other relief sought in Petitioner's Petition is not ruled upon at this time. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY SERVICES VS. CRYSTAL LEI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jun 23, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT BRYANT FU, TING POON, CRYSTAL LEI DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege a cause of action for non time-barred fraudulent transfer as to each defendant identifying the property transferred and held by each defendant within 7 years of the filing of the complaint. Plaintiff is not permitted to allege any other causes of action without leave of Court to do so. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
